CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, value VARCHAR(100));
SELECT * FROM test_strings WHERE value NOT LIKE 'b%';
SELECT * FROM test_strings WHERE value NOT LIKE 'a_%';
SELECT * FROM test_strings WHERE value NOT LIKE '%c';
SELECT * FROM test_strings WHERE value NOT LIKE '%a%';
SELECT * FROM test_strings WHERE value NOT LIKE 'd%' AND id > 1;
SELECT * FROM test_strings WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE value NOT LIKE 'e%');
SELECT ts1.id, ts1.value FROM test_strings ts1 JOIN test_strings ts2 ON ts1.id != ts2.id WHERE ts1.value NOT LIKE '%f%' AND ts2.value NOT LIKE '%g%';
SELECT * FROM (SELECT value FROM test_strings WHERE id = 1) AS subquery WHERE value NOT LIKE 'abc';
SELECT * FROM test_strings WHERE value NOT LIKE 'a%' OR value NOT LIKE 'e%';
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_strings WHERE value NOT LIKE '%abc%';
CREATE TABLE test_data (data_id INT, data_value VARCHAR(100));
SELECT td.data_id FROM test_data td WHERE td.data_value NOT LIKE 'abc%';
SELECT * FROM test_data WHERE data_value NOT LIKE '%m%' AND data_value NOT LIKE 'x%';
SELECT * FROM test_data WHERE data_value NOT LIKE (SELECT value FROM test_strings WHERE id = 2);
